title: Medical prescriptions is bullshit - I want my freedom to buy my own ashtma inhaler

transcript.text: Hello. I protest having to get prescriptions for medication.
We need freedom to be able to buy the medication we know we need for our bodies.
We don't need, we should not be required to pay a doctor who already makes way more money than you do
To agree with you and say, oh, okay, you have my permission to buy this medicine.
When I was in Europe, I was having Aslan. I needed a salbutamol inhaler.
I could not buy a salbutamol inhaler because I needed a prescription in all those countries in the EU.
And I suppose maybe I could have gotten a prescription anyway by like begging a pharmacist or paying a pharmacist off or something.
But it shouldn't be like that. People should have the freedom.
Why do I need to go and tell a doctor that I have asthma and have them have to like, what are they going to do?
Like, what if I'm not having an asthma attack at that time? Are they going to take a blood test?
It doesn't work that way.
They're just going to go, oh, you have asthma? Okay, here's your prescription.
Now go pay my secretary the $80 to see you.
The way it should be in the first world countries in America should be the way it is in third world countries.
In third world countries, you can get whatever you want.
There's pharmacies all over the place and it's great.
And the reason it is out there is because people can't afford to go and pay some doctor to get the prescription.
They just trust them to go out there and get the medicine.
This whole you having to get a prescription to get medicine causes more problems.
